articles.title: Harvard scientists turn a silicon chip into a DNA writing machine
articles.summary: Scientists have created a silicon chip that can write dozens of DNA sequences simultaneously using electricity and water-based enzymes, offering a cleaner alternative to conventional DNA manufacturing. The breakthrough…

articles.title: Ancient DNA reveals the mysterious collapse of Europe's megalith builders
articles.summary: DNA from a 5,000-year-old French megalithic tomb reveals that the people buried before and after a population collapse were genetically unrelated, pointing to a major migration after a devastating crisis. The shift…

articles.title: Scientists finally crack nature's secret for building better cancer drugs
articles.summary: Researchers have cracked the code behind bacteria's ability to naturally manufacture multiple versions of powerful anti-cancer drugs. The discovery could make it much easier to engineer new cancer treatments inspired by…

articles.title: Next‑generation membranes can refine crude oil using under half the energy of distillation
articles.summary: Oil refining is necessary for transforming raw, unusable crude oil into valuable goods like gasoline, diesel, jet fuel and petrochemical feedstocks. However, the usual distillation process is energy-intensive, spurring…

articles.title: Genetic crossovers defy chromosome-length model in male and female mice
articles.summary: A Cornell-led study is challenging a decades-old explanation for how chromosomes exchange genetic material within the biological process that forms eggs and sperm in mammals.

articles.title: SpaceX just launched the 1st-ever nuclear-powered commercial satellite
articles.summary: A satellite aboard SpaceX's Transporter-17 rideshare mission is the first commercially designed and operated spacecraft to harness nuclear energy in orbit.

articles.title: Hubble Captures Star-Studded Cluster
articles.summary: This image from NASA’s Hubble Space Telescope showcases Messier 3 (M3), one of the Milky Way galaxy’s most massive globular clusters, or spherical collections of gravitationally bound stars. Globular…

articles.title: Japanese company books 1,100 pounds of cargo space on SpaceX Starship mission to the moon
articles.summary: The Japanese company ispace is expanding its already extensive moon plans to include SpaceX's Starship megarocket.

articles.title: NASA begins funding hardware for 'Skyfall' Mars helicopter mission

articles.title: Warped spacetime reveals exoplanet far from its star
articles.summary: The Gaia and TESS space telescopes have discovered a distant Jupiter-like world - Gaia23bra b - by studying warped spacetime. The post Warped spacetime reveals exoplanet far from its star first appeared on EarthSky .

articles.title: Fossils found decades ago reveal extinct 3.5 million-year-old giant salamander species
articles.summary: In the late 1990s in the Ajimu region of Japan's Oita Prefecture, researchers discovered three fossilized vertebrae belonging to the Cryptobranchidae family of giant salamanders. These were embedded in the Tsubusugawa…

articles.title: A worm that lived half a billion years ago preferred turning right
articles.summary: Fossils of Spriggina floundersi provide the earliest evidence of animals favouring one side of the body over the other – a feature of nervous systems that we see in our own right- and left-handedness

articles.title: Bumblebees collect up to 7 times more toxic metals than honeybees
articles.summary: Bumblebees are picking up dramatically more toxic heavy metals than honeybees, even when both species forage in the same places. Researchers warn that this hidden pollution could quietly reduce their ability to find…

articles.title: Heat waves push tropical forests past photosynthesis limits across 57 million hectares
articles.summary: As heat waves continue one after another, we are feeling their effects on our own bodies: It becomes harder for us to function normally. Trees also have their limits when temperatures are too high. Above a certain…

articles.title: Camera traps reveal Chornobyl wildlife changed routines during Russian occupation
articles.summary: An international research team has for the first time investigated how an unfolding armed conflict influenced the behavior of wild animals. Using camera traps, the scientists documented how the Russian occupation of the…

articles.title: Collapse of AMOC ocean current may already be locked in
articles.summary: The fate of the Atlantic Ocean current that keeps Europe’s climate warm depends on our carbon emissions and the rate of ice melt from Greenland, but there is a chance that a shutdown is already inevitable

articles.title: El Nino powers up as forecasters predict historic strength and a rainier winter for the US South
articles.summary: An intensifying El Niño, nature's heat-releasing thermostat that spikes global temperatures, is heading to historically strong levels, the U.S. National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration said Thursday.

articles.title: Harmful ozone may have reached two-thirds of EU residents during record June heat wave
articles.summary: Two-thirds of the European Union's population may have been exposed to harmful levels of ozone pollution during last month's record-breaking heat wave, a report exclusively shared with AFP warned Thursday.

articles.title: China's pollution declines came at a cost

articles.title: Peter Shor’s algorithm could break the internet – but he's not worried
articles.summary: Few people have invented an algorithm with the potential to spark a worldwide crisis, so why is quantum computing pioneer Peter Shor so unconcerned? Karmela Padavic-Callaghan spoke to him to find out

articles.title: Scientists used AI to crack one of water's biggest mysteries

articles.title: Can the biggest problems in AI be solved by philosophy?
articles.summary: AI companies are hiring philosophy graduates to help them understand the nature of consciousness, whether it can be replicated and how their systems can be made better and more reliable

articles.title: AI just supercharged the race to find room temperature superconductors
articles.summary: Scientists have combined machine learning with quantum physics to discover two new superconductors and create a much faster way to search for many more. The technique could bring researchers significantly closer to the…

articles.title: Using mechanical vibrations instead of magnetic memory for quantum computing
articles.summary: Quantum computers still face limits when it comes to storing information. Researchers at ETH Zurich are now turning to mechanical vibrations rather than electromagnetic memory. Their new vibrating memory can store…

articles.title: The US Space Force just got a new electromagnetic weapon to jam adversary satellites
articles.summary: The U.S. Space Force has acquired one of its first publicly acknowledged offensive weapons, one that can blast adversary satellites with electromagnetic radiation.

articles.title: Heidelberg physicists just united two opposing quantum theories

articles.title: A surprisingly detailed look at the physics of a lugworm's poop
articles.summary: Feedback is delighted by a study of how many animals produce poop that echoes the look of the poop emoji – even the lugworm, which does it upside down
